rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Rescheduled Hearing On Petition To Enforce Foreign Subpoena
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 28, 2025, line 1. PETITIONER LYFT, INC.'S Petition To Enforce Foreign Subpoena. Petitioner Lyft, Inc.'s petition to enforce foreign subpoena is denied. Even if Lyft satisfied its burden that the discovery it seeks is permitted by the lawful consent exception to the Stored Communications Act, an issue which the court does not decide, Lyft's petition to enforce the second subpoena served on respondent Reddit, Inc. was filed after the mandatory deadline to compel compliance in two respects. First, because the second subpoena seeks the same information as the first subpoena, the second subpoena is an impermissible untimely effort to avoid the mandatory deadline to enforce the first subpoena, which had expired prior to service of the second subpoena. Second, Lyft's petition to enforce the second subpoena was filed and served after the mandatory deadline for enforcement of that subpoena. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion or Discovery Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(301/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: LEONIDAS DE CASTRO VS. DIEGO PEREZ-VARGAS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One; Requests For Admission, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 28, 2025, line 8. PLAINTIFF LEONIDAS CASTRO'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories-General, Set One; Requests For Admission, Set One; And Requests For Production Of Documents, Set One; And For Monetary Sanctions. Plaintiff Leonidas De Castro's motion to compel defendant Sanger Vicksburg Street LP (Vicksburg) to provide further responses to form interrogatories general set one, requests for admission set one, and requests for production of documents set one and for monetary sanctions is granted as to the the request for further responses and granted in part as to monetary sanctions. No later than June 6, 2025 Vicksburg must serve verified code-compliant responses without objections to all discovery requests covered by this motion and must pay sanctions in the amount of $2,000 to plaintiff. Vicksburg's request for monetary sanctions is denied. Vicksburg's hyper-technical, non-substantive, partially boilerplate and partially inaccurate objections to the discovery propounded on it and its refusal to provide any substantive responses is contrary to the self-executing nature of civil discovery and the important role of the meet and confer process to resolve civil discovery disputes. The primary purpose of civil discovery is to eliminate gamesmanship from the resolution of civil disputes, yet Vicksburg's conduct in responding to the discovery propounded on it is properly characterized as gamesmanship. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. rulings.Case Title: BREES COMPANY, INC. VS. OURA HEALTH OY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On the Law and Motion/Discovery calendar for May 28, 2025, line 5. DEFENDANTS OURA HEALTH OY, OURA RING, INC.'S MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION. Defendants Oura Health Oy and Ouraring Inc.'s motion to compel arbitration is granted. Plaintiff Brees Company, Inc. filed this lawsuit alleging, among other things, that defendants breached the option agreement. Plaintiff attached the option agreement to its complaint. The option agreement is a short document which references a 2018 equity plan in numerous places including in the title of the option agreement. The option agreement explicitly provides that: 1) the 2018 equity plan "govern[s] ... [plaintiff's] rights and obligations under the Option;" 2) "the Option and the shares ... are subject to restrictions on transfer as contained in ... the [2018] Plan; 3) plaintiff "acknowlege[s]" that he was provided with a copy of the [2018] Plan; and 4) plaintiff was advised to read the 2018 Plan "carefully." The foregoing shows that the 2018 equity plan is a key part of the Option Agreement. It is undisputed that there was and remains no 2018 equity plan and that the only equity plans that defendants have issued are a 2016 equity plan and a 2019 equity plan and that both of those plans provide for arbitration of all disputes relating to those plans. Under these circumstances, the references to a 2018 equity plan in the Option Agreement were a mistake and that those references were objectively intended to be to either the 2016 equity plan or the 2019 equity plan. For purposes of this motion, the court need not decide which of those two plans was objectively intended to be referenced in the Option Agreement. Based on the objective intentions of the parties, the court finds that there is a valid and enforceable agreement between the parties requiring the arbitration of the claims alleged by the plaintiff. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 301 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 301 Zoom ID 161 502 4290; Passcode 700956.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept301tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept301tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested.
